properties.title: The AI Oracle
properties.description: 3D models of Oracle Bones from the British Library collections (early chat GPT ;) This wrapper html page was created with the help of Claude AI.

properties.title: Enlightening Wisdom
properties.description: One of the first Hindu texts translated into a european language. Discovered through the Qalamos portal, a union catalogue of Asian and African script traditions in German memory institutions. Illustration features Nārada, a sage-divinity, famous in Hindu traditions as a travelling musician and storyteller, who carries news and enlightening wisdom. The image quality device included in this image is a further translation from physical to digital.

properties.title: Gamle Bybro
properties.description: A poscard of the gamle bybro (old town bridge) in Trondheim

properties.title: Thor Bach - Working in the living room
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Two people in motion, working. Thor Bach (on the right) studied electrical engineering at NTNU, and was a resistance fighter during World War II.

properties.title: Kristiansten Fortress
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: The picture shows buildings along the Nidelva, seen from Vollafallet. In the background Skrenten and the Winter Palace. Kristiansten Fortress is illuminated. Winter picture with snow.

properties.title: St Olaf at Sea
properties.description: The steam frigate "St. Olaf", photographed by Anders Beer Wilse in 1905.

properties.title: Trams and Palaces
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Stiftsgården, a royal palace, photographed by Anders Beer Wilse in 1902, is one of the largest wooden buildings in Northern Europe. The gardens behind it are now a public park.

properties.title: St Olaf statue
properties.description: The carved early 14th century sculpture of St Olaf, the patron saint of Norway. It originally stood in Bunge church on the Swedish island of Gotland.

properties.title: Harald Hardrada lands in York
properties.description: The landing of King Harald Hardrada near York, and the defeat of the Northumbrian army, depicted in the Life of St Edward the Confessor, c.1255.

properties.title: Harald Hardrada dies in battle
properties.description: The Battle of Stamford Bridge and the death of Harald Hardrada, depicted in the Life of St Edward the Confessor, c.1255.

properties.title: The world is a stage
properties.description: Explore and visualise the history of theatre through a curated collection of playbills and programmes.

properties.title: On the menu
properties.description: A dataset of 2,403 historical menu cards from the Berlin State Library, from about 1880 to 1913.

properties.title: On the menu
properties.description: Menu card, steamship "Kaiser Wilhelm der Grosse"

properties.title: Thor Bach - Escape from Trondheim
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: A photo album from Thor Bach's escape from Trondheim in May 1943.

properties.title: A game of cards
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: A photo of two men playing cards by photographer Hans Krum.

properties.title: Troll Stone
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Meadow of wood anemone flowers by a stream in a spruce forest. "Troll-stone" in the foreground. A troll stone, in Norwegian folk lore, trolls turn to stone in sunlight.

properties.title: Railways & Rivers
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: An image of the Flåmsbanen railway being built. It is wonderful how digitisation of images allow us to see past versions of places we currently visit.

properties.title: A Crowning for Trondheim Technical University
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: A postcard of the 1906 coronation procession in Munkegata on the way to the Cathedral. People stand along the street in front of Trondheim Technical University, now the City Hall.

properties.title: High speed crown
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Picture of the coronation procession in 1906. People have gathered in the street. In the background you can see the shops at Olav Tryggvasons gate 39-41. "Berlitz School", and "Hartmann A/S Jernvareforretning". A horse-drawn carriage drives past at full speed, perhaps the royal couple were in it.

properties.title: A Coronation
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: King Haakon VII and Queen Maud's coronation in Trondheim

properties.title: A Coronation
properties.description: King Haakon VII and Queen Maud's coronation in Trondheim

properties.title: Playing cards: Soroptimist International
properties.description: Pack of Soroptimist International playing cards in tartan box, produced by Scotland South Region to celebrate the SI centenary (1921-2021).

properties.title: Playing cards
properties.description: Lacquered box with sliding lid containing 92 hand painted circular papier Mughal Ganjifa or playing cards.

properties.title: collection of objects
properties.description: Five objects including a tobacco tin, “Capstan” Navy Cut’. ‘Regal Playing Cards’ full 52 deck of cards and joker.

properties.title: Ganjifa
properties.description: Ganjifa are playing cards or card games from India, Iran and some Arab countries. This set of ganjifa is one of two in the cabinet presented by George Lewis in 1726

properties.title: [The Popish Plot]
properties.description: Set of 52 playing cards depicting the Popish Plot, with suit-mark and value at top and a short description at foot; with blank backs

properties.title: 1930 (playing cards)
properties.description: Ben Nicholson, Playing Cards. On board

properties.title: Indecision
properties.description: Adelaide Claxton, 1858–1905, Indecision, undated

properties.title: OXO Jack of diamonds
properties.description: Jack of diamonds from a pack of playing cards with an advertisement for Oxo beef extract on the reverse.

properties.title: Pictorial cards
properties.description: Pictorial cards bearing the symbols of each of the four suits. Stipple engraving, 1818.

properties.title: Puir wee Laddie!!
properties.description: Puir wee Laddie!! : Ma mither winna gie me ony mair Oxo : It makes me grow oot o'ma claes. Four of diamonds from a pack of playing cards with an advertisement for Oxo beef extract on the reverse.

properties.title: Playing card
properties.description: Set of BOAC (British Overseas Airways Corporation) playing cards, in original card box

properties.title: Workshop for Non-Linear Architecture
properties.description: The Workshop for Non-Linear Architecture (WNLA) was a group of experimental artists and psychogeographers. The group was active in parts of Great Britain and Glasgow during the 1990s.
